empty.subtitle: There are no habits for today.
empty.title: No habits

terms.s1-intro: Dooit collects data related to your habits at two points: after completing the onboarding process and after completing the daily summary.
terms.s1-body: The collected data includes habit names, frequency of completion, and progress statistics. This data is used solely to analyze which habits users build most often and in what quantity. This analysis helps improve the app in line with users’ real needs.
terms.s2-body: All data is collected anonymously. Your username is not transmitted or stored outside your device. Habit data is labeled with a randomly generated identifier. Your data is not sold or shared with third parties.
terms.s3-body: You can permanently delete all your data at any time in the Settings tab. The data will be removed both from your device and from the Dooit database. Data deletion is irreversible.

message.delete-habit: Are you sure to remove the habit:
message.delete-execution: Are you sure to remove this execution?
message.delete-data: Are you sure? This will permanently delete all your habits, history, and account data.
message.change-goal:
Do you want to change the goal?
By default, it is set as the number of repetitions to be done within 4 weeks.
message.goal-reached: You've reached your goal! Want to push further and increase the limit to build an even stronger habit?
message.contact: Fill out the form to contact the app's author.
message.support:
Hey there!

I’m building Dooit on my own, with strong belief that it can truly improve everyday life. Every line of code was written to help you build habits that make a real difference.

If you feel that Dooit has supported you in some way, I’d appreciate it a lot if you contributed to its development - even with a virtual coffee. It’s a sign for me that what I’m doing matters.

~ Mikołaj Bryksa

contact.rate-limit: You've reached the daily limit (3 messages).

addStep.habitName: What do you want to do regularly?
addStep.repeatDays: On what days you want to do it?
addStep.repeatHours: What time do you want to do it?

tip.onboarding-habit-mindset: Think of a habit not as a big task — like working out or drinking 3 liters of water — but as the first small step that habit requires. For example: putting on your shoes or pouring a glass of water.
tip.onboarding-same-time: Habits stick most easily when you repeat them at the same time every day. Pick a time you can realistically commit to.
tip.now-repetitions-matter: In Dooit, only repetitions count. It doesn't matter how long you spend on a habit or what result you achieve — the only goal is to learn to consistently return to the planned activity.
tip.summary-daily-recap: Once you complete all habits planned for the day, Dooit will show you how many repetitions you managed to do and present a short recap of your day.
tip.habits-edit-and-goal: You can edit any habit at any time. The default goal is the number of repetitions calculated for 28 days — that's usually enough to build a habit. You can always increase it to make it even stronger!
tip.settings-overview: Here you can update your name used in the daily summary, change the app language, switch between dark and light mode, and toggle notifications on or off.

step2.which-habits: which habits you want to build

step3.accept-default: or keep the default repetition settings

start.subtitle: Hi, {{userName}}
start.title: Let’s get started
start.text:
For each habit,
just make one choice:
do it or skip it.

lastChance:
Count 5, 4, 3, 2, 1 and take action!

Mel Robbins shows that a quick countdown forces your brain to act immediately, before excuses can take over.

Take at least one small step today.

BJ Fogg emphasizes that even minimal actions build habits and increase your chances of success in daily life.

Imagine how your future self will thank you.

Hal Hershfield demonstrates that visualizing the consequences of your decisions increases the satisfaction of taking action.
done: One good habit leads to another.
Another brick in the wall of success.
Each action shapes who you become.
You’re building the best version of you.
Great choice, your routine is working.
skipped: One slip doesn’t erase your progress.
Learn from it, then move forward.
It’s a stumble, not a failure.
Your habit is stronger than this moment.
One skip is not the end.
It’s just a break, not a setback.
It’s just a pause, your journey continues.
intro: Another day is behind you, {{userName}}. Every time you show up here is a step in the right direction.
{{userName}}, you're here again — and that’s the key to success. Consistency makes the difference.
Habits are built one day at a time, {{userName}}. That’s why every day counts.
praise: The habit '{{bestHabit}}' is your highlight today. Great job.
'{{bestHabit}}' went the best for you today. That’s real progress.
'{{bestHabit}}' is really paying off. That’s something to be happy and proud of.
tip: With '{{worstHabit}}', start tomorrow with just one minute — getting started is already a success.
Tomorrow, with '{{worstHabit}}': one minute, no pressure. What matters is that you begin.
For '{{worstHabit}}', find a moment tomorrow for a small step. Even minimal action builds the habit.

summary.subtitle: All done today!
summary.title: Daily summary
summary.no-actions: Your progress summary will appear here once you complete at least one repetition of your chosen habit.
